disease_associations.disease: B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (FOXF1/FENDRR subtype with IGH::FENDRR rearrangement)

FOXF1.druggability_notes: FOXF1-mediated enhancer remodeling is proposed as a key target for modulating senescent cells to promote healthy aging. FOXF1 is highlighted as a potential therapeutic target in renal fibrosis (FOXF1-EZH2-DKK3 axis), pulmonary fibrosis (iron/ROS-FOXF1 feedback loop), and as a subtype-defining marker in B-ALL/T-ALL where early immunotherapy (blinatumomab) or targeted interventions may benefit patients. Identified as a potential biomarker/therapeutic target in osteosarcoma. No specific binding pocket or small-molecule drug class described.

disease_associations.disease: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

cellular_states: Cells undergoing G1/G2 cell cycle arrest
TP53.druggability_notes: Zinc/metal-binding transcription factor with a well-characterized sequence-specific DNA-binding domain and 3D structure available. The MDM2/MDM4 interaction axis is a well-known druggable interface for p53 reactivation (MDM2 and MDM4 noted as interactors; source 1 only for MDM2/MDM4). Pro-apoptotic activity is modulated by ASPP-family protein-protein interactions: PPP1R13B/ASPP1 and TP53BP2/ASPP2 are activating, while PPP1R13L/iASPP is inhibitory — all representing pharmacologically tractable PPI interfaces (both sources). The tetramerization domain is implicated as a structural target based on loss-of-function mutations in cancer affecting tetramerization ability (source 2). No explicit small-molecule binding pocket or drug class described beyond these PPI and structural observations.

disease_associations.disease: Non-small cell lung cancer / lung adenocarcinoma (EGFR-mutant)
Lung adenocarcinoma with uncommon EGFR G719A mutation
Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

EGFR.druggability_notes: EGFR is a well-established druggable tyrosine kinase target. Its cytoplasmic kinase domain (ATP-binding pocket) is the canonical site for small-molecule tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs), including quinazolinone derivatives and EGFR-TKIs such as afatinib. Amivantamab (anti-EGFR/MET bispecific antibody) plus chemotherapy is effective in EGFR-mutant NSCLC including uncommon G719A mutations. In silico molecular docking against EGFR (PDB 1XKK) identified plant-derived compounds (aspidinol, thunbergol) with binding affinities ~-8.6 kcal/mol. Oncogenic variants including EGFRvIII and ligand-responsive cancer variants drive constitutive signaling and represent additional therapeutic entry points. Pharmacologic EGFR inhibition suppresses microbiota-induced proliferative effects in pancreatic cancer.

disease_associations.disease: Hereditary breast cancer / Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome (BROVCA1)

BRCA1.druggability_notes: BRCA1-deficient (homologous recombination-deficient) tumors are sensitive to PARP inhibitors (olaparib, niraparib, fluzoparib) via synthetic lethality. BRCA1 possesses a zinc-finger (RING) domain conferring E3 ubiquitin ligase (transferase) activity; coiled-coil and RAD51-binding regions are also structurally relevant. Secondary intragenic mutations restoring BRCA1 function are a key PARPi resistance mechanism. In BRCA1/2-WT ccRCC, SLFN11-driven proteasomal degradation of BRCA1 creates PARP-1 dependency exploitable with alkylating agents (temozolomide, streptozocin) plus PARP inhibitors. No explicit small-molecule binding pocket beyond the RING domain is described in the source texts.

disease_associations.disease: Cutaneous squamous tumors / chemically induced skin carcinoma

cellular_states: Colorectal cancer cells (CRC; SW480 and related lineages)
KRAS.druggability_notes: Established proto-oncogene and major therapeutic target. Targetable by covalent inhibitor ARS-1620 (binds mutant KRAS G12C; off-target ALDH1A3 via C314). KRAS Q61 mutations (Q61H, Q61L) define distinct NSCLC subgroups. Structure-based KRAS-targeting peptides (Peptide-1) under investigation for colorectal cancer. Acquired KRAS inhibitor resistance mediated via ATF3-ASNS asparagine reprogramming, reversible by PERK inhibition. Nucleotide-binding (GDP/GTP) pocket and post-translational lipid modifications (palmitoylation, prenylation) are mechanistically relevant for targeting. BCR-mediated ubiquitination via LZTR1-CUL3-RBX1 provides an additional regulatory axis.
